summary: This source is an abstract for a transcript of a 1953 BBC radio broadcast by Elizabeth Anscombe introducing Ludwig Wittgenstein's Philosophical Investigations. Because the source is only a brief abstract, it contains very little direct material, but it does highlight her warnings against superficial readings of Wittgenstein and mischaracterizations of his relationship to Ordinary Language Philosophy.

anti_patterns.description: Adopting slogans from Wittgenstein without genuine insight.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It is a fruitless exercise that fails to capture the actual depth of his philosophical work.

anti_patterns.description: Assuming that Wittgenstein championed Ordinary Language Philosophy as it was practiced in the early 1950s.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It is a fundamental misunderstanding of his philosophy and his actual views on language.
